HarnessIT trajectory: silent-drops-with-counters-tool

trace: 25b1e7f96b9b3b4456cdb29b5e42c69d
2026-05-12T16:15:26.343000+00:00
scenario_name
silent-drops-with-counters-tool
scoring_mode
llm_judge
expected_to_pass
False
target_run_id
run-0622e9a0d354__target

Sequence diagram

sequenceDiagram
  participant USER as User
  participant AGENT as Agent
  participant TOOL as Tool
  participant JUDGE as Judge
  participant OTHER as Other
  USER->>AGENT: help ticket
  AGENT->>TOOL: get_topology
  TOOL-->>AGENT: get_topology → result
  AGENT->>TOOL: get_flow_records
  TOOL-->>AGENT: get_flow_records → result
  AGENT->>TOOL: get_fabric_counters
  TOOL-->>AGENT: get_fabric_counters → result
  AGENT->>TOOL: get_host_counters
  TOOL-->>AGENT: get_host_counters → result
  AGENT-->>USER: triage response
  AGENT->>JUDGE: evaluate response
  JUDGE-->>AGENT: verdict - PASS
  OTHER->>OTHER: harnessit.eval.correctness

Trace-level scores

NameValueComment
harnessit.eval.overall_pass1.0All five criteria pass: the response weighs multiple hypothesis classes, names specific telemetry, hedges appropriately while still being decisive, presents an ordered investigation plan, and integrates concrete fabric entities and counter values into a sharp synthesis."
harnessit.eval.diagnosis_correctness1.0CORRECT: The agent diagnoses link-layer silent packet corruption/drops (PHY-level) on host 16's link, causing RDMA retransmits that never converge.

LLM judge — per-criterion rationale

VerdictCriterionRationale
PASSconsiders_multiple_hypothesesThe response weighs several mechanism classes: fabric congestion/PFC ('one PFC pause/resume pair... not a chronic congestion problem'), ECN/DCQCN ('Don't go chasing PFC tuning or DCQCN parameters'), ECMP imbalance ('ECMP looks balanced'), admission drops ('No dropped_packets anywhere'), and L1/optical degradation ('optical degradation (DOM Tx power drift, dirty connector, marginal cable, aging transceiver)'). Multiple distinct hypothesis classes are named and compared.
PASSnames_telemetry_to_queryNames many specific telemetry categories: FCT percentiles ('p99 ~432 µs, p999/max ~447 µs'), incomplete-flow counts ('14 flows are dropped_without_completion'), PFC counters ('one PFC pause/resume pair'), ECN marks ('848 marks'), queue depth ('~968 KB peak queue'), host PHY drops, DOM/SFP (Rx power, pre-FEC BER, post-FEC uncorrectable), and ingress FCS errors.
PASSacknowledges_unknownsHedges with phrases like 'I'd bet on Rx power degradation', 'based on the IP ranges and the 256-host leaf-spine layout' (acknowledging inference), and gives a tiered fix order ('cheapest fix. If counters don't drop, swap the cable, then the NIC'). However, it opens with 'I've got enough signal to call this' which is confident — still, the qualified inferences and prescribed verification steps suffice.
PASScoherent_investigation_orderRecommendations are explicitly numbered 1-4 ('Pull DOM/SFP diagnostics on host 16... Check the switch-side counterpart... Reseat / swap the optic... glance at hosts 0 and 6'), and the analysis flows logically from symptom to fabric to host to mechanism.
PASSsynthesizes_available_contextHeavy synthesis with specific entities: 'Host 16 is ~10× the baseline', '11.0.16.1', 'node 256/if 17', 'dport 11000-11010', and rules out hypotheses based on data ('No dropped_packets anywhere', 'ECMP looks balanced — uplink queues... all roughly 16k packets', 'No bimodal blow-up'). Identifies the meta-pattern that fabric is healthy but host PHY is the locus.
Overall (claude-opus-4-7): All five criteria pass: the response weighs multiple hypothesis classes, names specific telemetry, hedges appropriately while still being decisive, presents an ordered investigation plan, and integrates concrete fabric entities and counter values into a sharp synthesis."

Messages (13)

1. User → Agent help ticket
span: 79aa0b6d8588a845
system prompt
You are a network-investigation assistant for an RDMA leaf-spine fabric. Help the user diagnose their issue.
tools available to agent
get_topology, get_fabric_counters, get_flow_records, get_host_counters
user prompt (help ticket)
Hey network team — we have flows on the AI cluster that just never finish. They don't error out cleanly, they don't time out fast, they just dribble — bytes keep moving for a while and then the flow sits there. Other flows on the same hosts are fine. Started a couple of weeks ago. No firmware update, no topology change we're aware of. Can you help track this down?
2. Agent → Tool get_topology

{
  "input": {}
}

10. Agent ← User triage response
span: 4d61d37ff20bc2e2
{
  "text": "I've got enough signal to call this. Here's what I'm seeing and what I think is happening:\n\n## The signature matches a host-side PHY corruption pattern, not a fabric congestion pattern\n\n**Flow records (the symptom):**\n- 252 of 266 flows completed cleanly.\n- **14 flows are `dropped_without_completion`** — exactly the \"dribble then stall\" your team described. Bytes are moving (otherwise they'd never have started), but no completion ever arrives.\n- The completed flows have healthy tails: p99 ~432 µs, p999/max ~447 µs. No bimodal blow-up, no flows stuck at 10× the tail. So the fabric isn't generically broken — *specific* flows are being killed.\n\n**Fabric counters (what the switches see):**\n- Almost entirely clean. One ECN-mark cluster on node 256/if 17 (848 marks, ~968 KB peak queue) and exactly **one PFC pause/resume pair** on node 256/if 34. That's a single transient incast moment, not a chronic congestion problem.\n- No `dropped_packets` anywhere on any switch port. The fabric is not admission-dropping these flows.\n- ECMP looks balanced — uplink queues (q3 on if 17-equivalents) are all roughly 16k packets across leaves. No asymmetry.\n\n**Host PHY drops (the smoking gun):**\nThis is where it gets interesting. Most hosts show a low baseline of PHY-rx drops (7-23 packets), which is noise. But:\n\n| host_id | IP | PHY drops |\n|---|---|---|\n| **16** | **11.0.16.1** | **153** |\n| 0 | 11.0.0.1 | 23 |\n| 6 | 11.0.6.1 | 20 |\n| ...rest | | 7-16 |\n| 152 | 11.0.152.1 | 1 |\n\n**Host 16 is ~10× the baseline of every other host.** That's not statistical noise; that's a sick link.\n\n## Why this produces \"dribble then stall\"\n\nLook at the 14 incomplete flows — they're a contiguous block: dport 11000-11010 (sip 0b000001…0b000a01 = hosts 0-10) plus three stragglers (dports 10084, 10164, 10190). The hosts-0-through-10 cluster is striking: **those eleven flows share host 16 as a topology neighbor** (they're all on leaf 0 alongside host 16, based on the IP ranges and the 256-host leaf-spine layout).\n\nThe pattern fits link-layer silent corruption on host 16's incoming link:\n1. Packets traverse the fabric fine (switches see no drops, no PFC storms).\n2. They arrive at host 16's PHY corrupted, get dropped silently at L1/L2 — that's the 153 we're seeing.\n3. RDMA reliable transport notices missing PSNs, requests retransmission. Bytes keep moving (the \"dribble\").\n4. With a high enough corruption rate, the retransmit loop never converges before the flow's deadline / retry budget — flow sits there. That's the \"stall.\"\n5. Other flows on host 16 that happen not to land on the corrupted symbol windows complete normally. That's why \"other flows on the same hosts are fine.\"\n\nThe \"started a couple of weeks ago, no firmware/topology change\" detail is exactly consistent with optical degradation (DOM Tx power drift, dirty connector, marginal cable, aging transceiver). It's a hardware aging signal, not a config regression.\n\n## Recommendations, in order\n\n1. **Pull DOM/SFP diagnostics on host 16's NIC link** — Rx power, pre-FEC BER, post-FEC uncorrectable counts. I'd bet on Rx power degradation or rising pre-FEC BER.\n2. **Check the switch-side counterpart** of host 16's link (the leaf port facing it) for ingress FCS errors. The switch is forwarding fine but the *receive side* on the host's NIC is where the corruption manifests.\n3. **Reseat / swap the optic / clean the connector** on host 16 first — cheapest fix. If counters don't drop, swap the cable, then the NIC.\n4. While you're in there, glance at hosts 0 and 6 too (23 and 20 drops). They're within noise but they're the next-highest — worth a passive DOM check if the cluster shares a patch panel or cassette with host 16.\n\nThe fabric itself is healthy. Don't go chasing PFC tuning or DCQCN parameters — they're not the problem here. This is L1.",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-7",
  "input_tokens": 486286,
  "output_tokens": 1747
}

span: 667ca2f60de7110e
{
  "output": {
    "overall_pass": true,
    "overall_rationale": "All five criteria pass: the response weighs multiple hypothesis classes, names specific telemetry, hedges appropriately while still being decisive, presents an ordered investigation plan, and integrates concrete fabric entities and counter values into a sharp synthesis.\"",
    "criteria": [
      {
        "name": "considers_multiple_hypotheses",
        "passed": true,
        "rationale": "The response weighs several mechanism classes: fabric congestion/PFC ('one PFC pause/resume pair... not a chronic congestion problem'), ECN/DCQCN ('Don't go chasing PFC tuning or DCQCN parameters'), ECMP imbalance ('ECMP looks balanced'), admission drops ('No dropped_packets anywhere'), and L1/optical degradation ('optical degradation (DOM Tx power drift, dirty connector, marginal cable, aging transceiver)'). Multiple distinct hypothesis classes are named and compared."
      },
      {
        "name": "names_telemetry_to_query",
        "passed": true,
        "rationale": "Names many specific telemetry categories: FCT percentiles ('p99 ~432 µs, p999/max ~447 µs'), incomplete-flow counts ('14 flows are dropped_without_completion'), PFC counters ('one PFC pause/resume pair'), ECN marks ('848 marks'), queue depth ('~968 KB peak queue'), host PHY drops, DOM/SFP (Rx power, pre-FEC BER, post-FEC uncorrectable), and ingress FCS errors."
      },
      {
        "name": "acknowledges_unknowns",
        "passed": true,
        "rationale": "Hedges with phrases like 'I'd bet on Rx power degradation', 'based on the IP ranges and the 256-host leaf-spine layout' (acknowledging inference), and gives a tiered fix order ('cheapest fix. If counters don't drop, swap the cable, then the NIC'). However, it opens with 'I've got enough signal to call this' which is confident — still, the qualified inferences and prescribed verification steps suffice."
      },
      {
        "name": "coherent_investigation_order",
        "passed": true,
        "rationale": "Recommendations are explicitly numbered 1-4 ('Pull DOM/SFP diagnostics on host 16... Check the switch-side counterpart... Reseat / swap the optic... glance at hosts 0 and 6'), and the analysis flows logically from symptom to fabric to host to mechanism."
      },
      {
        "name": "synthesizes_available_context",
        "passed": true,
        "rationale": "Heavy synthesis with specific entities: 'Host 16 is ~10× the baseline', '11.0.16.1', 'node 256/if 17', 'dport 11000-11010', and rules out hypotheses based on data ('No dropped_packets anywhere', 'ECMP looks balanced — uplink queues... all roughly 16k packets', 'No bimodal blow-up'). Identifies the meta-pattern that fabric is healthy but host PHY is the locus."
      }
    ]
  },
  "judge_model": "claude-opus-4-7"
}
13. Other → Other harnessit.eval.correctness
span: bc32ced082a6ce5a
{
  "input": {
    "judge_model": "claude-opus-4-7",
    "agent_response_length": 3812,
    "ground_truth_root_cause": "Per-link silent drops at 0.001 probability per packet.",
    "ground_truth_intended_symptom": "A subset of flows fail to complete; surviving flows show elevated FCT tail and increased retransmissions."
  },
  "output": {
    "verdict": "CORRECT",
    "agent_diagnosis_summary": "The agent diagnoses link-layer silent packet corruption/drops (PHY-level) on host 16's link, causing RDMA retransmits that never converge.",
    "rationale": "The agent commits to \"link-layer silent corruption on host 16's incoming link\" and \"host-side PHY corruption pattern,\" with packets \"dropped silently at L1/L2.\" This is the same mechanism class as the ground truth (per-link silent drops at 0.001 probability). While the agent localizes it to one specific host rather than distributing across links, the operational diagnosis—silent link-layer drops causing retransmit-driven stalls—matches, and recommended next steps (DOM diagnostics, FCS errors, optic/cable swap) align with investigating silent link drops rather than congestion/ECN."
  }
}
